project.name: TradingAgents
project.description: TradingAgents: Multi-Agents LLM Financial Trading Framework. A multi-agent system that simulates real trading firms with specialized analyst, researcher, trader, and risk-management agents collaborating via LangGraph to produce trading decisions.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Files excluded from Docker build context.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Example environment variables — LLM API keys and overrides.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project README — overview, install, and usage.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point — interactive Typer app for running TradingAgents.

nodes.name: main.py
nodes.summary: Programmatic entry point — instantiates TradingAgentsGraph and propagates a ticker.

nodes.name: smoke_structured_output.py
nodes.summary: End-to-end smoke for structured-output agents against a real LLM provider.

nodes.name: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Shared pytest fixtures that prevent CI hangs when API keys are absent.

nodes.name: test_api_key_env.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the canonical provider->env-var mapping and the CLI key-prompt helper.

nodes.name: test_capabilities.py
nodes.summary: Unit tests for the LLM capability table.

nodes.name: test_checkpoint_resume.py
nodes.summary: Test checkpoint resume: crash mid-analysis, re-run resumes from last node.

nodes.name: test_dataflows_config.py
nodes.summary: Config isolation: get/set must not leak nested-dict references.

nodes.name: test_env_overrides.py
nodes.summary: Tests for TRADINGAGENTS_* env-var overlay onto DEFAULT_CONFIG.

nodes.name: test_memory_log.py
nodes.summary: Tests for TradingMemoryLog — storage, deferred reflection, PM injection, legacy removal.

nodes.name: test_ollama_base_url.py
nodes.summary: Tests for OLLAMA_BASE_URL env-var override across CLI and client paths.

nodes.name: test_safe_ticker_component.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the ticker path-component validator that blocks directory traversal.

nodes.name: test_signal_processing.py
nodes.summary: Tests for the shared rating heuristic and the SignalProcessor adapter.

nodes.name: test_structured_agents.py
nodes.summary: Tests for structured-output agents (Trader and Research Manager).

nodes.name: fundamentals_analyst.py
nodes.summary: Fundamentals analyst agent — analyzes company financials and earnings.

nodes.name: market_analyst.py
nodes.summary: Market analyst agent — produces technical/price analysis.

nodes.name: news_analyst.py
nodes.summary: News analyst agent — summarizes ticker-relevant news.

nodes.name: sentiment_analyst.py
nodes.summary: Sentiment analyst — multi-source sentiment analysis for a target ticker.

nodes.name: social_media_analyst.py
nodes.summary: Backwards-compatibility shim for the renamed module.

nodes.name: portfolio_manager.py
nodes.summary: Portfolio Manager: synthesises the risk-analyst debate into the final decision.

nodes.name: research_manager.py
nodes.summary: Research Manager: turns the bull/bear debate into a structured investment plan for the trader.

nodes.name: bear_researcher.py
nodes.summary: Bear researcher agent — argues the pessimistic case for trading decisions.

nodes.name: bull_researcher.py
nodes.summary: Bull researcher agent — argues the optimistic case for trading decisions.

nodes.name: aggressive_debator.py
nodes.summary: Aggressive risk debator — pushes for higher-risk positions.

nodes.name: conservative_debator.py
nodes.summary: Conservative risk debator — pushes for risk-averse positions.

nodes.name: neutral_debator.py
nodes.summary: Neutral risk debator — balances aggressive vs conservative views.

nodes.name: schemas.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic schemas used by agents that produce structured output.

nodes.name: trader.py
nodes.summary: Trader: turns the Research Manager's investment plan into a concrete transaction proposal.

nodes.name: agent_states.py
nodes.summary: TypedDict definitions for LangGraph agent state.

nodes.name: core_stock_tools.py
nodes.summary: Core stock-data tools exposed to agents.

nodes.name: memory.py
nodes.summary: Append-only markdown decision log for TradingAgents.

nodes.name: rating.py
nodes.summary: Shared 5-tier rating vocabulary and a deterministic heuristic parser.

nodes.name: structured.py
nodes.summary: Shared helpers for invoking an agent with structured output and a graceful fallback.

nodes.name: interface.py
nodes.summary: Unified data-fetch interface used by analyst agents.

nodes.name: reddit.py
nodes.summary: Reddit search fetcher for ticker-specific discussion posts.

nodes.name: analyst_execution.py
nodes.summary: Manages parallel/sequential execution of analyst nodes.

nodes.name: checkpointer.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph checkpoint support for resumable analysis runs.

nodes.name: conditional_logic.py
nodes.summary: Conditional routing logic for LangGraph state transitions.

nodes.name: propagation.py
nodes.summary: Drives state propagation through the LangGraph pipeline.

nodes.name: reflection.py
nodes.summary: Reflection layer — agents review past decisions to learn from outcomes.

nodes.name: setup.py
nodes.summary: Builds and configures the LangGraph state machine for the trading pipeline.

nodes.name: signal_processing.py
nodes.summary: Extract the 5-tier portfolio rating from the Portfolio Manager's decision.

nodes.name: trading_graph.py
nodes.summary: Top-level TradingAgentsGraph orchestrator — wires all agents together via LangGraph.

nodes.name: base_client.py
nodes.summary: Base class for all LLM client wrappers.

nodes.summary: Declarative per-model capability table for OpenAI-compatible providers.

nodes.summary: Return shared model options for a provider and selection mode.

nodes.summary: ChatOpenAI with normalized content output and capability-aware binding.

layers.name: Entry Points
layers.description: Top-level scripts that bootstrap a TradingAgents run.

layers.description: Interactive Typer/Rich CLI for running TradingAgents.

layers.name: Orchestration (LangGraph)
layers.description: Top-level TradingAgentsGraph + LangGraph state machine, routing, propagation, reflection, and checkpointing.

layers.name: Analyst Agents
layers.description: Specialist analyst LLM agents (fundamentals, market, news, sentiment, social) that gather and interpret signals.

layers.name: Researcher Agents
layers.description: Bull and bear researcher agents that debate the trade thesis.

layers.name: Manager Agents
layers.description: Research and portfolio managers — orchestrate debate and make final allocation decisions.

layers.name: Trader Agent
layers.description: Trader agent — produces the final BUY/HOLD/SELL trade signal.

layers.name: Risk Management Agents
layers.description: Aggressive, conservative, and neutral debators evaluating risk before execution.

layers.name: Agent Utilities
layers.description: Shared agent infrastructure: state schemas, memory, structured-output helpers, and tool wrappers.

layers.name: Data Sources
layers.description: Adapters for market and social data: Alpha Vantage, YFinance, Reddit, StockTwits, technical indicators.

layers.name: LLM Clients
layers.description: Provider-specific LLM client wrappers (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Azure) with capability detection and a factory.

layers.name: Tests
layers.description: Pytest test suite covering analysts, providers, env overrides, and signal processing.

layers.name: Scripts
layers.description: Standalone utility scripts (smoke tests, dev helpers).

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Docker build and compose definitions for containerized deployment.

layers.name: Project Config
layers.description: Project metadata, dependency manifests, and environment templates.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: README, changelog, integration plans, learning guides.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start with the README to understand what TradingAgents is — a multi-agent LLM framework that simulates a trading firm with analysts, researchers, risk debators, and a portfolio manager collaborating to make trade decisions.

tour.title: Programmatic Entry Point
tour.description: main.py shows the simplest way to run the system: instantiate TradingAgentsGraph and call propagate(ticker, date). This is the file new contributors should run first.

tour.title: Top-Level Orchestrator
tour.description: TradingAgentsGraph wires every agent together via LangGraph. Read this to see the high-level pipeline: analysts → researchers → manager → risk debate → trader.

tour.title: Configuration & Env Overrides
tour.description: default_config.py is the single source of truth for runtime settings. .env.example documents the LLM API keys and TRADINGAGENTS_* env-var overrides users can flip without editing code.

tour.title: LLM Client Factory
tour.description: The factory picks a provider (OpenAI / Anthropic / Google / Azure / DeepSeek / Ollama) based on config and constructs the right client, with capability detection (vision, function calling, reasoning effort).

tour.title: Analyst Agents
tour.description: Five specialist analysts run in parallel: fundamentals, market, news, sentiment, social-media. Each pulls from data sources and emits a report into shared state.

tour.title: Data Sources
tour.description: Analysts call into pluggable data adapters. yfinance is the free default; alpha_vantage_* provides paid alternatives. The interface module is the unified API analysts use.

tour.title: Bull vs Bear Debate
tour.description: Researchers debate the analyst reports. The bull researcher argues for opening the position; the bear pushes back. Their debate transcript feeds the research manager.

tour.title: Trader → Risk Debate → Portfolio Manager
tour.description: The trader proposes a trade. Three risk debators (aggressive / conservative / neutral) stress-test it. The portfolio manager makes the final call.

tour.title: State, Memory & Reflection
tour.description: agent_states.py defines the LangGraph TypedDict state shared by every node. memory.py persists the decision log. reflection.py lets the system learn from past trades.

tour.title: Signal & Checkpoint
tour.description: signal_processing extracts the BUY/HOLD/SELL decision from the manager output. checkpointer enables resuming a crashed LangGraph run from the last successful step.

tour.title: Interactive CLI
tour.description: cli/main.py is the user-facing Typer/Rich app — interactive prompts for ticker, date, provider, and analyst selection. Use this for ad-hoc runs.

tour.title: Tests
tour.description: The pytest suite under tests/ covers analyst execution, env overrides, structured-output, checkpoint resume, and per-provider regressions.

tour.title: Containerized Deployment
tour.description: Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml package TradingAgents for reproducible runs. .env.enterprise.example documents the multi-region API-key setup.
